overview: Sultai Ouroboroid leverages mana acceleration and land synergy to outpace opponents in Standard while establishing a resilient board presence. By cycling through Nature's Rhythm and deploying Llanowar Elves, the deck ensures consistent land drops to cast larger threats efficiently. The strategy focuses on maintaining tempo through creature recursion and incremental advantage until the late game closes out matches.
win_conditions: Deploying large, resilient creatures that outvalue opponent's removal
Generating incremental card and mana advantage through land synergies
Establishing an unblockable board state with token generation and recursion

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two mana sources by turn two
Early plays like Llanowar Elves or Nature's Rhythm
A mix of lands and low-cost creatures to maintain tempo
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, Llanowar Elves, Nature's Rhythm, or early interactive threats.
Prioritize hands that can play a land and a Llanowar Elves on turn one, or a land and Nature's Rhythm on turn two.
mulligan_criteria: Three or more lands without early plays
Hands missing interaction or ramp in the first three turns
Mulligan hands with 0-1 lands, multiple high-cost finishers without ramp, or hands lacking early interaction.

early_game: Prioritize playing Llanowar Elves on curve to accelerate green mana
Use Nature's Rhythm to fetch lands or draw cards, ensuring land drops
Deploy Badgermole Cub to establish early board presence and enable synergies
Turn 1: Play a land, tap Llanowar Elves if possible.
Turn 2: Ramp into Nature's Rhythm or deploy a 2-drop threat.
Sequence lands to ensure Sultai colors by turn 2-3, prioritizing fetches/shocks if available.
Use Nature's Rhythm to draw cards while slowing opponent's land drops, setting up a stable mana base for midgame recursion.
mid_game: Transition into larger threats while maintaining mana advantage
Use recursion and token generation to replace lost creatures
Apply pressure while holding up interaction for opponent's threats
Preserve mana for interaction until turn 3.
avoid over-committing to recursion if the board is unstable.
Manage hand size by trading cards for board presence, and use Nature's Rhythm to cycle into answers or threats when resources are tight.
Commit threats when you have removal ready or the opponent is tapped out.
Hold back if facing sweepers or heavy removal.
late_game: Cast high-impact threats that leverage accumulated mana
Use land synergies and card draw to outlast opponent's resources
Close out games with unblockable attackers and board wipes if necessary
Deploy resilient threats that can't be easily removed, or chain recursion engines to generate lethal damage or card advantage.
Use late-game Nature's Rhythm to draw into win conditions or finish off low-life opponents with recursive threats.
Go all-in when you have board control, the opponent is low on removal, or you've established a recursive loop that can't be answered.
Commit when you can force unfavorable trades or when your opponent is tapped out for interaction.

key_cards.name: Badgermole Cub
key_cards.role: Early board presence and synergy enabler
key_cards.notes: Play on curve to establish a body and trigger land or token synergies; use its abilities to stabilize against early aggression.

key_cards.name: Nature's Rhythm
key_cards.notes: Cast to search for basic lands or draw cards, ensuring you never fall behind on land drops while maintaining hand size.

key_cards.name: Llanowar Elves
key_cards.notes: Deploy immediately to tap for green mana, enabling early land drops and accelerating into mid-game threats.

sequencing_tips: Hold up mana for interaction before committing to land drops
Prioritize playing mana dorks before casting ramp spells to maximize efficiency
Sequence land fetches to ensure color fixing for U/B/G spells
Common mistake: Over-ramping into a single win condition.
Always maintain a board presence and keep interactive spells in hand.
Unique interaction: Nature's Rhythm can be used to tutor for lands or trigger landfall synergies.
Badgermole Cub's recursion can be exploited by graveyard hate, so protect it or have backup plans.
Practice timing your recursion to avoid giving opponents free interaction windows.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early blockers and use ramp to outpace their curve
Apply early pressure with creatures to force interaction before they stabilize
Race with efficient threats while using card advantage to outlast their removal

Use split spells, cheap threats, or layered effects to force counterspells. Trade resources to burn through their protection, then play around the last one. Use graveyard recursion to bypass counters and apply pressure from unexpected angles.

Apply early pressure and use spot removal on key ramp creatures. Disrupt their mana development with land hate or counters, and avoid trading into their late-game finishers. Stabilize quickly and close out before they reach their power spike.

Win the resource war by prioritizing card draw, recursion, and targeted removal. Control the graveyard and force unfavorable trades. Out-value them in the late game by maintaining a steady stream of threats and answers.

common_threats: Watch for Izzet's counterspells and cheap removal to time your plays around them.
Note when Landfall decks start dropping multiple lands per turn to anticipate their power spike.
Track graveyard contents for recursion threats or combo pieces, and adjust your removal priorities accordingly.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: deck archetype and signature card roles
